title: Within-stint ranking with hard negatives near the actual stop
summary: Adjacent ranking literature fits this label structure well: each Driver x Race x Stint is a short sequence where one or a few laps are much more stop-imminent than the others.
rationale: Adjacent ranking literature fits this label structure well: each Driver x Race x Stint is a short sequence where one or a few laps are much more stop-imminent than the others. Ranking within the stint is more aligned than global logloss, and hard negatives from the last few non-stop laps before the pit event should be more informative than easy early-stint negatives.
implementation_hint: Group by `Race_Year x Driver x Stint` (or `Race x Driver x Stint` if needed). Use CatBoost `QuerySoftMax`/`PairLogitPairwise` or LightGBM `lambdarank`/`rank_xendcg`; assign higher relevance to the positive lap and optionally to laps 1-3 before a stop. Sample or weight negatives by proximity to the stop to focus training on the decision boundary. Validation trap: never split a group across folds.
expected_effect: Can lift AUC by improving the ordering of borderline laps within the same stint without requiring a full survival pipeline.
risk: If some stints have no positive event or ambiguous boundaries, ranking data construction can get noisy; keep a simple fallback classifier in the ensemble.
